<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

This PR migrates the project's build/CI tooling from Poetry/Black to uv/ruff, restructures `pyproject.toml` to PEP 621, and replaces the pytest-poetry workflow with a new CI workflow and updated publish workflow. It refactors `betapert`'s distribution classes into a shared `_BasePERT` base, reworks PPF log-space fallback broadcasting, replaces the skew formula, and adds `entropy`, `munp`, and `fit` (maximum-likelihood estimation) functions along with an updated `argcheck`. Extensive new tests cover degenerate modes, fitting, moments/entropy, vectorized parameters, and property-based invariants. Documentation (README, LICENSE) and a new docs figure-generation script were also added.

### Sequence Diagram(s)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant User
  participant PERT as pert/mpert
  participant Funcs as betapert.funcs
  participant Beta as scipy.stats.beta

  User->>PERT: ppf(q, mini, mode, maxi, lambd)
  PERT->>Funcs: ppf(q, ..., fallback=FALLBACK)
  Funcs->>Funcs: _calc_alpha_beta(mini, mode, maxi, lambd)
  Funcs->>Beta: ppf(q, alpha, beta)
  Beta-->>Funcs: NaN (numerical failure)
  Funcs->>Funcs: _ppf_fallback_log_space(q, alpha, beta)
  Funcs->>Beta: brentq root search on logcdf
  alt root found
    Beta-->>Funcs: x_normalized
  else root search fails
    Funcs->>Beta: ppf(clamped q, alpha, beta)
    Beta-->>Funcs: fallback x
  end
  Funcs-->>PERT: x (scaled to mini/maxi)
  PERT-->>User: x
```

**Possibly related PRs**
- [hbmartin/betapert#4](https://github.com/hbmartin/betapert/pull/4): Both PRs modify `_calc_alpha_beta`'s scalar-vs-array return behavior for all-equal array inputs in `betapert/funcs.py`.

**Suggested labels:** `Review effort 3/5`

</details>

## Code Review

This pull request refactors the package to use a shared base class `_BasePERT` for both `PERT` and `ModifiedPERT` distributions, transitions the build system from Poetry to `uv`, and introduces several key features such as maximum likelihood parameter fitting (`fit`), closed-form entropy and non-central moments, support for degenerate modes, and robust vectorized parameter broadcasting. A critical bug was identified in the `_ppf_fallback_log_space` function, where returning a scalar based solely on `np.isscalar(q)` discards broadcasted array results when other parameters are arrays. A code suggestion was provided to ensure a scalar is returned only if all inputs are scalars.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The [consumer version of Gemini Code Assist on GitHub](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-repo-code) is being sunset. Starting **June 18, 2026**, new organization installations will be blocked, and all code review activity will officially cease on **July 17, 2026**.
> For more details on the timeline and next steps, please review the [Help Documentation](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/deprecations/consumer-code-review).

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

There is a bug in the broadcasting logic when `q` is a scalar but any of the distribution parameters (`mini`, `mode`, `maxi`, `lambd`) are arrays. Because `np.isscalar(q)` is `True`, the function will return `results[0]` (a single scalar), discarding the rest of the broadcasted array. To fix this, we should only return a scalar if all inputs are scalars.

```suggestion
    # Returns scalar only if all inputs are scalars, otherwise returns array
    is_scalar = (
        np.isscalar(q)
        and np.isscalar(mini)
        and np.isscalar(mode)
        and np.isscalar(maxi)
        and np.isscalar(lambd)
    )
    return results[0] if is_scalar else results
```

P2: The `uv_build` version constraint `>=0.8.17,<0.9.0` pins builds to the old 0.8.x series (latest: 0.8.24). The uv docs recommend `>=0.11.26,<0.12` — loosening the upper bound lets builders get recent bug fixes, performance improvements, and metadata validation. Non-uv frontends (e.g., `python -m build`) will always use the `uv_build` PyPI package, so they'd be stuck on 0.8.x.

The uv docs recommend `>=0.11.26,<0.12` — loosening the upper bound lets builders get recent bug fixes, performance improvements, and metadata validation. Non-uv frontends (e.g., `python -m build`) will always use the `uv_build` PyPI package, so they'd be stuck on 0.8.x.</comment>

```text
<file context>

-requires = ["poetry-core"]
-build-backend = "poetry.core.masonry.api"
+requires = ["uv_build>=0.8.17,<0.9.0"]
+build-backend = "uv_build"
 
</file context>
```

</details>

```suggestion
requires = ["uv_build>=0.11.26,<0.12"]
```

Fix only still-valid issues, skip the > rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. > > In @.github/workflows/ci.yml around lines 1 - 33, The CI workflow needs > hardening by tightening token scope and avoiding credential persistence in the > checkout step. Also replace the mutable action references used > by actions/checkout and astral-sh/setup-uv with pinned full commit SHAs so the > workflow is not dependent on moving tags. > ``` > >

> betapert/funcs.py (1)
> > `150-156`: _🚀 Performance & Scalability_ | _🔵 Trivial_ | _⚡ Quick win_ > > **`ppf` discards valid direct results when any element is NaN.** > > When `_beta_ppf` contains a mix of valid and NaN values, the whole array is recomputed via `_ppf_fallbacks[fallback](...)`, discarding the already-correct closed-form values from `scipy.stats.beta.ppf` for entries that didn't need a fallback. This wastes the more numerically precise vectorized `beta.ppf` result for unaffected elements and forces every element through the slower per-element `brentq` root-finding path. > > Consider only replacing the NaN entries: > >
> ♻️ Proposed fix > > ```diff > def ppf(q, mini, mode, maxi, lambd=4, *, fallback=None): > alpha, beta = _calc_alpha_beta(mini, mode, maxi, lambd) > _beta_ppf = mini + (maxi - mini) * scipy.stats.beta.ppf(q, alpha, beta) > - # Use fallback if any values are NaN > - if fallback is not None and np.any(np.atleast_1d(np.isnan(_beta_ppf))): > - return _ppf_fallbacks[fallback](q, mini, mode, maxi, lambd) > - return _beta_ppf > + nan_mask = np.isnan(np.atleast_1d(_beta_ppf)) > + if fallback is not None and np.any(nan_mask): > + fallback_result = np.atleast_1d(_ppf_fallbacks[fallback](q, mini, mode, maxi, lambd)) > + return np.where(nan_mask, fallback_result, np.atleast_1d(_beta_ppf)) > + return _beta_ppf > ``` >
> >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the > rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate. > > In `@betapert/funcs.py` around lines 150 - 156, The ppf function currently > recomputes the entire output through the fallback path whenever any element of > _beta_ppf is NaN, which discards valid scipy.stats.beta.ppf results. Update ppf > so it detects NaN entries in the _beta_ppf result and only substitutes those > positions using _ppf_fallbacks[fallback], while preserving the already-correct > values. Use the ppf symbol and the _ppf_fallbacks lookup to patch the array in > place rather than replacing the whole result. > ``` > >
> > > >
> >

Inline comments: In @.github/workflows/publish.yml: - Around line 8-36: Harden the publish workflow by tightening trusted-publishing access in the test and pypi-publish jobs. Pin all action references in this workflow (actions/checkout, astral-sh/setup-uv, pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish) to verified commit SHAs instead of mutable tags, set persist-credentials: false on both checkout steps, and scope the test job’s permissions to contents: read. Also disable or explicitly control setup-uv caching in the Install uv steps to reduce cache-poisoning risk while keeping the publish flow functional. In `@betapert/__init__.py`: - Around line 31-32: Introduce a shared type alias for the repeated _unpack return annotation so the same tuple[FloatOrArray, FloatOrArray, FloatOrArray, FloatOrArray] type is defined once and reused everywhere it appears. Update the _unpack declarations and any other matching signatures in betapert to reference the new alias (for example, a single UnpackedArgs name) instead of repeating the full tuple type, keeping the existing function structure unchanged. In `@betapert/funcs.py`: - Around line 321-329: The Nelder-Mead setup in `fit` uses a fixed absolute `fatol=1e-10`, which can be too strict for large `data.size` and cause false non-convergence. Update the `scipy.optimize.minimize` call to make `fatol` scale with the objective magnitude (for example based on `negative_log_likelihood(x0)`) or enable `adaptive=True` for Nelder-Mead, while keeping the existing `xatol`/`result.success` handling in place. In `@README.md`: - Around line 127-131: The version requirement is inconsistent across the author blurb, the stated runtime requirement, and the packaging metadata. Update the README entry under Authors, the “Requires Python…” text, and the requires-python value in pyproject.toml so they all agree on the same minimum Python version; use the package-level metadata and any related docs like the README section to keep the wording consistent. --- Outside diff comments: In @.github/workflows/ci.yml: - Around line 1-33: The CI workflow needs hardening by tightening token scope and avoiding credential persistence in the checkout step. In the workflow job, add an explicit permissions block with the minimum required access, and update the Checkout code step in the CI job to disable persisted credentials. Also replace the mutable action references used by actions/checkout and astral-sh/setup-uv with pinned full commit SHAs so the workflow is not dependent on moving tags. In `@betapert/funcs.py`: - Around line 150-156: The ppf function currently recomputes the entire output through the fallback path whenever any element of _beta_ppf is NaN, which discards valid scipy.stats.beta.ppf results. Update ppf so it detects NaN entries in the _beta_ppf result and only substitutes those positions using _ppf_fallbacks[fallback], while preserving the already-correct values. Use the ppf symbol and the _ppf_fallbacks lookup to patch the array in place rather than replacing the whole result. ```

_🔒 Security & Privacy_ | _🟠 Major_ | _🏗️ Heavy lift_

**Harden the publish pipeline — it has trusted-publishing (`id-token: write`) access.**

This workflow builds and publishes to PyPI with no additional gating beyond the `test` job. Static analysis flags several supply-chain gaps that matter more here than in a plain CI workflow:
- All 5 `uses:` references (`actions/checkout@v4`, `astral-sh/setup-uv@v6` ×2, `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1`) are pinned to mutable tags rather than commit SHAs. A compromised or hijacked tag on any of these (as happened with `tj-actions/changed-files` in 2025) would run with the ability to influence the artifact that gets published.
- Neither checkout step sets `persist-credentials: false`.
- `setup-uv` has caching enabled by default, which carries a cache-poisoning risk on a workflow with elevated permissions.
- The `test` job has no explicit `permissions:` block (defaults to the repo's broad `GITHUB_TOKEN` scope).

Recommend pinning all actions to full commit SHAs, adding `persist-credentials: false` on checkout, and scoping `permissions: contents: read` on the `test` job.





<details>
<summary>🔒 Suggested hardening (illustrative — replace with actual verified SHAs)</summary>

```diff
   test:
     name: Run tests before publishing
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    permissions:
+      contents: read
     steps:
       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          persist-credentials: false
       - name: Install uv
         uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
         with:
           python-version: "3.13"
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
  test:
    name: Run tests before publishing
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.13"
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: uv sync --locked
      - name: Run tests
        run: uv run pytest --random-order

  pypi-publish:
    name: Upload release to PyPI
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      # IMPORTANT: this permission is mandatory for trusted publishing
      id-token: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
      - name: Build distributions
        run: uv build
      - name: Publish package distributions to PyPI
        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

_📐 Maintainability & Code Quality_ | _🔵 Trivial_ | _💤 Low value_

**Consider a shared type alias for the repeated `_unpack` return type.**

The identical `tuple[FloatOrArray, FloatOrArray, FloatOrArray, FloatOrArray]` annotation is repeated three times. A single alias (e.g. `UnpackedArgs`) defined once and reused would reduce duplication and simplify future changes.

<details>
<summary>♻️ Proposed refactor</summary>

```diff
+UnpackedArgs = tuple[FloatOrArray, FloatOrArray, FloatOrArray, FloatOrArray]
+
+
 class _BasePERT(scipy.stats.rv_continuous):
     `@staticmethod`
-    def _unpack(args: tuple) -> tuple[FloatOrArray, FloatOrArray, FloatOrArray, FloatOrArray]:
+    def _unpack(args: tuple) -> UnpackedArgs:
         raise NotImplementedError
```
</details>





Also applies to: 97-99, 131-132

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@betapert/__init__.py` around lines 31 - 32, Introduce a shared type alias for
the repeated _unpack return annotation so the same tuple[FloatOrArray,
FloatOrArray, FloatOrArray, FloatOrArray] type is defined once and reused
everywhere it appears. Update the _unpack declarations and any other matching
signatures in betapert to reference the new alias (for example, a single
UnpackedArgs name) instead of repeating the full tuple type, keeping the
existing function structure unchanged.
```

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_🩺 Stability & Availability_ | _🔵 Trivial_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Fixed absolute `fatol` may hinder convergence for larger datasets.**

`fatol=1e-10` is an absolute tolerance on the (summed) negative log-likelihood, whose magnitude scales with `data.size`. For large samples (e.g. `tests/test_fit.py`'s 50,000-point fits), tiny floating-point noise in a log-likelihood on the order of 1e4–1e5 can make it hard for Nelder-Mead's simplex value-spread to ever drop below `1e-10`, risking spurious non-convergence (`RuntimeError`) that isn't really about the fit quality. Combined with the hard `np.inf` cliff for out-of-bounds params, this makes the optimizer's success criterion somewhat fragile for atypical inputs.

Consider scaling `fatol` relative to the objective's magnitude (e.g. `abs(negative_log_likelihood(x0)) * 1e-10`) or enabling `adaptive=True` for Nelder-Mead.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```

rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

uses a fixed absolute `fatol=1e-10`, which can be too strict for large
`data.size` and cause false non-convergence. Update the
`scipy.optimize.minimize` call to make `fatol` scale with the objective
magnitude (for example based on `negative_log_likelihood(x0)`) or enable
`adaptive=True` for Nelder-Mead, while keeping the existing
`xatol`/`result.success` handling in place.
```

<!-- cr-comment:v1:daffbf488943c4326392074e -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

There is a bug in the broadcasting logic when `q` is a scalar but any of the distribution parameters (`mini`, `mode`, `maxi`, `lambd`) are arrays. Because `np.isscalar(q)` is `True`, the function will return `results[0]` (a single scalar), discarding the rest of the broadcasted array. To fix this, we should only return a scalar if all inputs are scalars.

```suggestion
    # Returns scalar only if all inputs are scalars, otherwise returns array
    is_scalar = (
        np.isscalar(q)
        and np.isscalar(mini)
        and np.isscalar(mode)
        and np.isscalar(maxi)
        and np.isscalar(lambd)
    )
    return results[0] if is_scalar else results
```

The uv docs recommend `>=0.11.26,<0.12` — loosening the upper bound lets builders get recent bug fixes, performance improvements, and metadata validation. Non-uv frontends (e.g., `python -m build`) will always use the `uv_build` PyPI package, so they'd be stuck on 0.8.x.

_🔒 Security & Privacy_ | _🟠 Major_ | _🏗️ Heavy lift_

**Harden the publish pipeline — it has trusted-publishing (`id-token: write`) access.**

This workflow builds and publishes to PyPI with no additional gating beyond the `test` job. Static analysis flags several supply-chain gaps that matter more here than in a plain CI workflow:
- All 5 `uses:` references (`actions/checkout@v4`, `astral-sh/setup-uv@v6` ×2, `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1`) are pinned to mutable tags rather than commit SHAs. A compromised or hijacked tag on any of these (as happened with `tj-actions/changed-files` in 2025) would run with the ability to influence the artifact that gets published.
- Neither checkout step sets `persist-credentials: false`.
- `setup-uv` has caching enabled by default, which carries a cache-poisoning risk on a workflow with elevated permissions.
- The `test` job has no explicit `permissions:` block (defaults to the repo's broad `GITHUB_TOKEN` scope).

Recommend pinning all actions to full commit SHAs, adding `persist-credentials: false` on checkout, and scoping `permissions: contents: read` on the `test` job.





<details>
<summary>🔒 Suggested hardening (illustrative — replace with actual verified SHAs)</summary>

```diff
   test:
     name: Run tests before publishing
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    permissions:
+      contents: read
     steps:
       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          persist-credentials: false
       - name: Install uv
         uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
         with:
           python-version: "3.13"
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
  test:
    name: Run tests before publishing
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.13"
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: uv sync --locked
      - name: Run tests
        run: uv run pytest --random-order

  pypi-publish:
    name: Upload release to PyPI
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      # IMPORTANT: this permission is mandatory for trusted publishing
      id-token: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
      - name: Build distributions
        run: uv build
      - name: Publish package distributions to PyPI
        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->
